item.title: 关注周五（2022 年 5 月 27 日） | Follow Friday (27 May 2022)
item.description:
朋友们五一快乐！ 这是 DEV 上又一个精彩的一周，我想知道：最近在 DEV 上你最喜欢关注的作者是谁，为什么？

格式化提示：要使用关注按钮填充卡片，请使用流动标签语法{% embed https://... %}并插入您最喜欢的作者个人资料的 URL。

通过@标记另一个您认为值得关注的社区成员（以及为什么您喜欢他们的帖子！）来加入#FollowFriday 乐趣⤵️

博主和程序员 - Apr 24 4月24日



原文:

Happy Friday, friends! 🎉

It's been another great week of content on DEV, and I'm wondering: who's your favorite author to follow here on DEV lately, and why?

Formatting tip: to populate a card with a follow button, use the liquid tag syntax {% embed https://... %} and insert the URL of your favorite author's profile.

Join in on the #FollowFriday fun by @ tagging another community member who you think is worth a follow (and why you love their posts!) ⤵️

Blogger and Programmer - Apr 24
item.author: erinposting

item.title: Vim：初学者的初学者指南 | Vim: A Beginner's Guide From A Beginner
item.description:
那么，让我们来试试吧！

我已经使用 Vim 大约一个半月了，我一直很喜欢它（虽然第一周有点痛苦）。

在本文中，我将讨论为什么要使用 Vim、如何设置它以及 Vim 的一些基础知识。

为什么要使用 Vim？

现在，不要与我争吵，我不会将 Vim 与任何其他文本编辑器进行比较。 我只是告诉你 Vim 能提供什么。

  • 完全可定制- 这意味着你可以完全控制，有些人甚至让它看起来像 vscode（我个人喜欢保持最小化）
  • 生产力——嗯，一开始，你的生产力会下降，但是当你开始习惯 Vim 和它的键绑定时，它肯定会提高
  • 键盘驱动（如果你设置了偏好，你可以使用鼠标）——这对你来说可能是也可能不是好事
  • 看起来不错

新病毒

如果您还没有听说过 Neovim，请不要惊慌，这没什么可怕的。 通常人们不会说他们使用 Neovim，他们只是称它为 Vim。 这真的没有太大区别，只是 Neovim 对插件有更多的支持。 此外，两者的默认键绑定和命令是相同的，因此，如果您学习 Neovim 键绑定，您也可以使用 Vim。

安装

对于 Mac 和 Linux 机器，根据您的包管理器执行以下任一操作：

或者

您可以通过键入vi或vim来运行它。

以防万一，您在不知道键绑定的情况下立即打开它并且现在吓坏了，只需输入:q! 并按回车退出。

视窗

对于 Windows 用户，您有两种选择：

  1. 您可以使用 wsl（Linux 的 Windows 子系统）并按照与 Linux 系统相同的步骤操作。 我不会在这里详细介绍安装wsl。
  2. 使用 Powershell（不是 Windows Powershell），您可以从 Microsoft 商店下载它，然后使用 scoop（Windows 的包管理器）安装 Neovim：

要安装 scoop，请在终端中键入以下内容：

如果它告诉您安装额外的buckets ，请也这样做。

关闭并重新打开您的终端，您可以通过键入nvim 。

以防万一，您在不知道键绑定的情况下立即打开它并且现在吓坏了，只需输入:q! 并按回车退出。

第一眼

这是它现在的样子：

现在看起来不太好，是吗？

让我们配置它！

配置

现在，有很多很好的预配置 neovim 设置，例如NvChad和AstroNvim ，但我建议您至少先尝试自己的配置。 我个人不使用预配置的，我更喜欢使用自己的配置。

您必须首先为 neovim 创建一个配置文件。

对于 Linux 和 Mac 用户，配置文件应该在~/.config/nvim中创建。

对于 Windows 用户，应在~/AppData/Local/nvim创建配置文件。

在各自的目录中打开终端。 创建文件，其名称应为init.vim 。 在这个文件中，我们将使用 vimscript 来编写我们的配置。

快速说明：您也可以使用init.lua代替init.vim ，更多关于这一点的内容，在本文中我将使用init.vim 。

现在，我们将使用 Neovim 配置 Neovim（第一次听起来很奇怪）。

基本规则

转到终端内的上述目录，然后键入nvim init.vim并按 enter。

快速说明：如果您只键入nvim而不提及任何文件，它将打开类似于欢迎页面的内容。 如果你输入nvim file.md并且 file.md 不存在，Neovim 将打开它，如果你保存它，文件将被创建。 同样的规则也适用于 Vim。

现在，不需要点击任何东西，忘记你有一个鼠标，一旦你面前打开了那个令人生畏的文本编辑器，只需按i （没有 shift 键，没有 ctrl 键，只是简单的小i ），这让你输入 insert模式。 现在，您可以正常编辑文件了。 要退出插入模式或更确切地说是 Vim 中的任何模式，请按 Escape 或 esc 键，这不会退出编辑器，只会让您返回正常模式（默认模式）。

您现在也可以使用箭头键浏览文件。

快速提示：在普通模式下，左移按'h'，右移按'l'，上移按'k'，下移按'j'。

在开始使用插件之前，让我们设置一些基本规则/设置：

 set number " Sets line numbers set autoindent " Sets auto indentation set tabstop = 2 " Sets tabstop set shiftwidth = 2 " For proper indentation set smarttab " Affects how tab key presses are interpreted set softtabstop = 2 " Control how many columns Vim uses when you hit tab key set mouse = a " This lets you use your mouse set wrap " Sets up line wrapping

快速提示：如果您不了解某个设置的作用，只需打开 Vim 并在正常模式下输入:help <property_name> ，例如对于 shiftwidth，请执行:help shiftwidth 。

接下来，按 esc 键退出插入模式。 现在输入:wq （无需单击任何地方或按所有这些 ctrl 和 shift 键）并按 Enter，这将写入文件（基本上，它将保存文件）然后退出编辑器。

如果您只使用:w ，它只会写入文件，不会退出编辑器。

如果您只使用:q ，它会退出编辑器，但如果您有未保存的更改，它不会让您这样做，在这种情况下，请执行:q! 如果您想要我在这里没有提到的房产，只需搜索它，我相信您会找到它。

让我们现在设置插件！

设置插件

现在，有一堆插件管理器（Vim/Neovim 需要插件管理器来安装插件），但对于本教程，我们将坚持使用vim-plug 。

对于 Unix/Linux，您可以使用以下命令安装 vim-plug：

 sh -c 'curl -fLo "${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}"/nvim/site/autoload/plug.vim --create-dirs \ https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim'

对于 Windows（Powershell），您可以使用以下命令安装 vim-plug：

 iwr -useb https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim | ` ni " $( @ ( $env :XDG_DATA_HOME, $env :LOCALAPPDATA )[ $null -eq $env :XDG_DATA_HOME] ) /nvim-data/site/autoload/plug.vim" -Force

查看他们的安装说明了解更多信息。

现在在neovim中再次打开init.vim ，导航到文件末尾，也可以使用方向键，这次不要按i ，按o ，会换行，进入插入模式。 现在输入以下内容（在基本规则之后）：

 call plug#begin () " Your plugins go here call plug#end ()

快速说明：您可以使用"在 vimscript 中编写单行注释。例如" This is a comment 。

我们所有的插件都在call plug#begin和call plug#end之间。

以下是我使用的一些插件：

  • 您可以像这样列出您的插件：

     call plug#begin Plug 'neoclide/coc.nvim' , { 'branch' : 'release' } Plug 'andweeb/presence.nvim' Plug 'itchyny/lightline.vim' Plug 'ryanoasis/vim-devicons' Plug 'preservim/nerdtree' | \ Plug 'Xuyuanp/nerdtree-git-plugin' Plug 'jiangmiao/auto-pairs' Plug 'copilot.vim' call plug#end

    快速说明：永远记住，在安装插件之前，请在安装前查看其文档，因为有时他们可能对插件的设置有特殊说明

    完成后，保存文件并退出 Neovim（记住，使用:wq并按回车键）。 重新打开 Neovim。 要安装这些插件，请在正常模式下键入:PlugInstall并按 Enter。 完成后，这将打开另一个窗格并开始安装所有插件。 关闭窗格 ( :q )，然后也关闭 init.vim。 这会退出 Neovim，现在，重新进入，所有插件将开始工作。

    快速提示：要在窗格之间移动，请按 ctrl+w，然后按h移动到左侧窗格，然后按l移动到右侧窗格

    什么是 LSP？

    好吧，LSP（语言服务提供商）是为您提供语言支持的东西（编辑器中的语言支持意味着它指出您的错误并在为特定语言编码时提供建议）。 它也是从 0.5 版本开始内置的 neovim。 我们不会在这里配置本地 LSP，而是使用 coc.nvim。

    什么是 Coc.nvim？

    它带来了自动完成、建议和其他一些东西。 建议初学者使用，因为设置不多。 我们之前已经将 coc.nvim 作为插件安装了。

    好吧，有了这个，我们可以为我们使用的语言安装 coc 的支持。 例如，如果要安装对 python 的支持，只需执行:CocInstall coc-python 。 大多数语言都会以 coc-[language-name] 的形式提供支持，但为了确保您应该只用 google 搜索它，您会很容易找到它。

    键绑定

    现在，花点时间感到自豪，你已经克服了对 vim 的恐惧。

    在 vim/neovim 中，您可以设置自己的键绑定，而不是已经存在的键绑定。

    例如，假设我想设置一个键绑定来关闭 Neovim 中的文件。 所以，一般来说，我必须输入:q 。

    为此，请在init.vim文件的末尾（在call plug#end之后）添加以下内容：

    这是什么意思？

    在这里，在<Cq>中，“C-”代表 ctrl 键，然后是您想要与其结合使用的任何键。 例如， <Cq>表示 ctrl+q， <Ck>表示 ctrl+k。

    在两者之间， <Cq>和<CR>是此键映射所针对的命令。

    因此， nnoremap <Cq> :q<CR>意味着您必须按 ctrl+q ，这与键入:q然后按 enter 相同。

    由于我们安装了插件，比如 nerdtree，它们为我们带来了自己的命令，例如现在您可以访问:NERDTreeToggle和:CocInstall 。

    所以这里有一些你可以为 nerdtree 设置的键绑定：

     nnoremap < leader > n : NERDTreeFocus < CR > nnoremap < C - n > : NERDTree < CR > nnoremap < C - t > : NERDTreeToggle < CR > nnoremap < C - f > : NERDTreeFind < CR >

    此键绑定来自他们在 GitHub 存储库上的自述文件，但您可以自由更改它，如果您愿意，可以随意更改。 请记住，不要有冲突的键绑定。

    不过你一定想知道，那边的<leader>是什么。 这代表\ ，因此nnoremap <leader>n :NERDTreeFocus<CR>意味着您必须输入 \n 并且它将运行:NERDTreeFocus命令。

    书呆子树

    为 nerdtree 设置键绑定后，您可以按 ctrl+t 打开它，您可以像使用箭头键或 [h, j, k, l] 键浏览文件一样导航它。 要删除或重命名文件，请按m ，它将打开一个菜单，列出如何操作。 要创建文件，请导航到您希望它所在的节点，然后按m打开菜单。

    差不多就这样结束了。

    主题

    您可以像使用插件一样安装主题。 你可以搜索一下主题，那里有很多。 我个人不使用任何主题，因为我的终端主题对我来说已经足够好了。

    不用担心，我在本文末尾列出了一些资源供您找到一个好的主题。

    早些时候，我说过你也可以把你的配置放在init.lua中。 许多人更喜欢使用 Lua，因为它比 Vimscript 更符合逻辑。

    如果你想将你的init.vim转换为init.lua ，你可以查看 Olivier Roques 的这篇文章。

    最后的样子

    如果您已按照本文中的所有内容进行操作，您的init.vim现在应该如下所示：

     " Basic Rules set number set autoindent set tabstop = 2 set shiftwidth = 2 set smarttab set softtabstop = 2 set mouse = a set encoding = UTF -8 set wrap " Plugins call plug#begin Plug 'neoclide/coc.nvim' , { 'branch' : 'release' } Plug 'andweeb/presence.nvim' Plug 'itchyny/lightline.vim' Plug 'ryanoasis/vim-devicons' Plug 'preservim/nerdtree' | \ Plug 'Xuyuanp/nerdtree-git-plugin' Plug 'jiangmiao/auto-pairs' Plug 'copilot.vim' call plug#end " Keymaps nnoremap < leader > n : NERDTreeFocus < CR > nnoremap < C - n > : NERDTree < CR > nnoremap < C - t > : NERDTreeToggle < CR > nnoremap < C - f > : NERDTreeFind < CR >

    这是它现在的样子：

    深吸一口气，感受一下成就感，大功告成。 现在，继续试验它，让 Vim 成为你自己的。

    这是来自 devhints 的vim 备忘单。 您可以查看更多命令。

    一些技巧

    • 不要放弃 Vim，至少尝试一两个星期，然后再决定扔掉它。 我知道一开始它很烦人，但很多人在使用后最终喜欢它。
    • 不要关闭 Vim，不要在不退出 Vim 的情况下退出终端，有时会损坏文件
    • 不要使用 ctrl+z，出于某种原因冻结我的 Vim
    • 您可以通过在事物前面加上!来使用 shell 命令。
    • 提高你的打字速度，它通常也会帮助你。
    • 使用别名来加快您的开发过程。
    • 实践。 这是习惯 Vim 的最佳方式。

    一些有用的工具

    • 使用目录跳线，因此您不必一直保持cd ing。 我用z
    • 您可以为 Linux 和 Mac 设备使用终端多路复用器。 虽然从未听说过 Powershell 上的终端多路复用器

    更多资源

    • 如果有人想指出我错过的东西或任何其他资源，或者只是对我无法正确解释的东西有疑问，请在下面评论。

      希望你喜欢它！



原文:

Vim, one of the oldest contestants in the text editor war, ever since its release in 1991. It has been a friend to many and a foe to many, wanna find out what it will be for you? Then, let's try it out!

I have been using Vim for about a month and a half now, and I have been loving it (the first week was a bit painful though).

In this article, I'll be discussing why to use Vim, how to set it up, and some basics of Vim.

Why To Use Vim?

Now, don't fight with me, I am not comparing Vim with any other text editor. I am just telling you what Vim has to offer.

  • Fully customizable - It means you have full control, some people even make it look like vscode (I personally like to keep it minimal though)
  • Productivity - Well, in the beginning, your productivity would decline but as you start getting used to Vim and its keybindings, it will increase for sure
  • Keyboard-driven (you can use a mouse though if you set the preference) - This may or may not be a good thing for you
  • Looks cool

Neovim

Don't freak out if you haven't heard of Neovim, it's nothing scary. Neovim is just an implementation of Vim but more focused on extensions and plugins, a bit less customizable than Vim. Often people don't say they use Neovim, they just call it Vim. It really doesn't make much of a difference, just that Neovim has more support for plugins. Also, the default keybindings and commands for both are the same, so, if you learn Neovim keybindings you can as well use Vim.

Installation

Mac & Linux

For Mac & Linux machines, do either of the following depending on your package manager:

or

Vim (not Neovim) is installed by default on most Linux machines. You can run it by typing vi or vim.

Just in case, you opened it right now without knowing the keybindings and are now freaking out, just type :q! and press enter to exit.

Windows

For windows users, you have two options:

  1. You can use wsl (Windows subsystem for Linux) and follow the same steps as for Linux systems. I won't go into details about installing wsl here.
  2. Use Powershell (not Windows Powershell), you can download it from the Microsoft store, and install Neovim using scoop (A package manager for windows):

To install scoop, type the following into your terminal:

winget is another package manager that comes pre-installed in windows nowadays.

If it tells you to install additional buckets, do it too.

Close and reopen your terminal and you can open Neovim by typing nvim.

Just in case, you opened it right now without knowing the keybindings and are now freaking out, just type :q! and press enter to exit.

First Look

This is how it looks right now:

Right now that doesn't look very good, does it?

Let's configure it!

Configuration

Now, there are many good pre-configured neovim setups out there like NvChad and AstroNvim, but I advise you to first at least try out your own configuration. I personally don't use the pre-configured ones, I prefer to use my own configuration.

You will have to create a configuration file for neovim first.

For Linux & Mac Users, the config file should be created at ~/.config/nvim.

For Windows Users, the config file should be created at ~/AppData/Local/nvim.

(In the above ~ represents the home directory)

Open your terminal in your respective directories. Create the file, its name shall be init.vim. In this file, we will be using vimscript to write our configuration.

Quick note: You can also use init.lua instead of init.vim, more about that towards the end, in this article I will be following with init.vim.

Now, we are going to configure Neovim using Neovim (sounded pretty weird to me the first time).

Basic Rules

Go to the directory mentioned above inside your terminal, and type nvim init.vim and press enter.

Quick note: If you just type nvim without mentioning any file, it will open up something like a welcome page. If you type nvim file.md and file.md does not already exist, Neovim will open it and if you save it the file will be created. Same rules apply to Vim too.

Now, no need to click anything, forget you have a mouse, once that intimidating text editor is open in front of you, just press i (no shift key, no ctrl key, just plain simple little i), this lets you enter insert mode. Now, you can edit your file normally. To exit insert mode or rather any mode in Vim in general, press the escape or esc key, this doesn't exit the editor just returns you to normal mode (the default mode).

You can also use arrow keys to navigate the file for now.

Quick Note: when in normal mode, to move left press 'h', to move right press 'l', to move up press 'k', and to move down press 'j'.

Before jumping on to plugins let's set up some basic rules/settings:

set number " Sets line numbers
set autoindent " Sets auto indentation
set tabstop=2 " Sets tabstop
set shiftwidth=2 " For proper indentation
set smarttab " Affects how tab key presses are interpreted
set softtabstop=2 " Control how many columns Vim uses when you hit tab key
set mouse=a " This lets you use your mouse
set wrap " Sets up line wrapping

Quick tip: If you don't understand what a certain setting does, just open Vim and in normal mode type, :help <property_name>, for example for shiftwidth, do :help shiftwidth.

Next, press esc key to exit insert mode. Now type :wq (no need for clicking anywhere or pressing all those ctrl & shift keys) and press enter, this will write to file (basically, it will save the file) and then exits the editor.

If you use just type :w, it just writes to the file, it doesn't exit the editor.

If you use just type :q, it exits the editor, but it won't let you do that if you have unsaved changes, in that case, do :q!, this will revert all unsaved changes and exit the editor forcefully.

If you want a property I haven't mentioned here, just search it up, I am sure you will find it.

Let's set up the plugins now!

Setting Up Plugins

Now, there are a bunch of plugin managers (Vim/Neovim requires plugin managers to install plugins) out there but for this tutorial, we will stick with vim-plug.

For Unix/Linux, you can install vim-plug with:

sh -c 'curl -fLo "${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}"/nvim/site/autoload/plug.vim --create-dirs \ https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim'

For Windows(Powershell), you can install vim-plug with:

iwr -useb https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim |` ni "$(@($env:XDG_DATA_HOME, $env:LOCALAPPDATA)[$null -eq $env:XDG_DATA_HOME])/nvim-data/site/autoload/plug.vim" -Force

Check out their install instructions for more.

Now open init.vim in neovim again, and navigate to the end of the file, you can also use arrow keys, don't press i this time, press o, this will make a new line and enter you to insert mode. Now type in the following (after the basic rules):

call plug#begin() " Your plugins go here call plug#end()

Quick note: You can use " to write single-line comments in vimscript. For example " This is a comment.

All our plugins will go in between the call plug#begin and call plug#end.

Here are some of the plugins I use:

  • coc.nvim - I will elaborate a bit more on this later
  • presence.nvim - a plugin to let you show that you are using Vim on Discord
  • lightline.vim - a light and configurable statusline/tabline plugin for Vim
  • nerdtree - a tree explorer plugin for vim, more about this later
  • nerdtree-git-plugin - a plugin to show git status in nerdtree
  • vim-devicons - a plugin to show icons for your files
  • auto-pairs - completes your parentheses, brackets and quotes
  • copilot.vim - If you have GitHub Copilot, then you can use it with this plugin

You can list your plugins like this:

call plug#begin Plug 'neoclide/coc.nvim', {'branch': 'release'}
Plug 'andweeb/presence.nvim'
Plug 'itchyny/lightline.vim'
Plug 'ryanoasis/vim-devicons'
Plug 'preservim/nerdtree' |
 \ Plug 'Xuyuanp/nerdtree-git-plugin'
Plug 'jiangmiao/auto-pairs'
Plug 'copilot.vim' call plug#end

Quick note: Always remember, before you install a plugin, do check out its docs before installing, because sometimes they may have special instructions on the setup for a plugin

Once it's done, save the file and exit Neovim (remember, use :wq and press enter). Reopen Neovim. To install these plugins, type :PlugInstall in normal mode and press enter. This will open another pane and start installing all the plugins, once it's done. Close the pane (:q), then close init.vim too. This exits Neovim, now, re-enter and all the plugins will start to work.

Quick Tip: to move between panes, press ctrl+w, and then h to move to the left pane and l to move to the right pane

Coc.nvim & LSP

What is LSP?

Well, LSP (Language Service Provider) is the thing that gives you language support (language support in an editor means it points out your mistakes and offers suggestions while coding for a specific language). It is also built-in neovim since version 0.5. We won't be configuring the native LSP here, instead we will use coc.nvim.

What is Coc.nvim?

Coc.nvim is an ecosystem that builds beyond the native LSP, meaning you don't have to configure anything. It brings autocomplete, suggestions, and some other stuff. It's recommended for beginners as there is not much setup. We already installed coc.nvim earlier as a plugin.

Well, with that out of the way, we can install the support of coc for our used languages. For example, if you want to install the support for python, just do :CocInstall coc-python. Most languages will have their support as coc-[language-name] but just to be sure you should just google it, you will easily find it.

Keybindings

Now, take a moment and feel proud, you have conquered a bit of the fear of vim.

In vim/neovim, you can set up your own keybindings apart from the ones already there.

For example, let's say I want to set up a keybinding for closing a file in Neovim. So, in general, I would have to type :q.

To do that at the end of your init.vim file (after the call plug#end) add the following:

What does this mean?

nnoremap is a declaration that you are setting a keybinding, so you will have to put that before declaring any keybindings.

Here, in <C-q>, the "C-" stands for the ctrl key, followed by whatever key you want to use in combination with it. For example, <C-q> means ctrl+q and <C-k> means ctrl+k.

In between, <C-q> & <CR> is the command for which this keymapping is.

<CR> represents the enter key. So, nnoremap <C-q> :q<CR> means you have to press ctrl+q and it will be the same as typing :q and then pressing enter.

Since, we installed plugins, like nerdtree, they brought their own commands for us, for example now you have access to :NERDTreeToggle and :CocInstall.

So here are some keybindings you can setup for nerdtree:

nnoremap <leader>n :NERDTreeFocus<CR>
nnoremap <C-n> :NERDTree<CR>
nnoremap <C-t> :NERDTreeToggle<CR>
nnoremap <C-f> :NERDTreeFind<CR>

This keybinding is from their readme on their GitHub repo, but you are free to change it, you can make to if you prefer, whatever suits you. Just keep in my mind, not to have conflicting keybindings.

You must be wondering though, what is that <leader> over there. That stands for \, so nnoremap <leader>n :NERDTreeFocus<CR> is means you have to type \n and it will run :NERDTreeFocus command.

NERDTree

After setting up the keybindings for nerdtree, you can press, ctrl+t to open it up, you can navigate it just like you would a file with arrow key or [h, j, k, l] keys. To delete or rename a file, press m, and it will open up a menu listing out how to do it. To create a file, navigate to the node you want it to be in, and then press m to open up the menu.

That about wraps it up.

Themes

You can install Themes the same way you did with plugins. You can search about the themes, there are many out there. I personally don't use any theme because my terminal's theme is good enough for me.

Don't worry, I have listed some resources for you to find a good theme at the end of this article.

Lua & Vimscript

Earlier, I stated that you could have put your config in init.lua too. Many people prefer to use Lua because it's more logical than Vimscript.

If you wish to convert your init.vim to init.lua, you can check out this article by Olivier Roques.

Final look

If you have followed everything in this article, your init.vim should look something like this now:

" Basic Rules set number
set autoindent
set tabstop=2
set shiftwidth=2
set smarttab
set softtabstop=2
set mouse=a
set encoding=UTF-8
set wrap " Plugins call plug#begin Plug 'neoclide/coc.nvim', {'branch': 'release'}
Plug 'andweeb/presence.nvim'
Plug 'itchyny/lightline.vim'
Plug 'ryanoasis/vim-devicons'
Plug 'preservim/nerdtree' |
 \ Plug 'Xuyuanp/nerdtree-git-plugin'
Plug 'jiangmiao/auto-pairs'
Plug 'copilot.vim' call plug#end " Keymaps nnoremap <leader>n :NERDTreeFocus<CR>
nnoremap <C-n> :NERDTree<CR>
nnoremap <C-t> :NERDTreeToggle<CR>
nnoremap <C-f> :NERDTreeFind<CR>

This is how it looks now:

Take a deep breath, and feel a sense of accomplishment, you are done. Now, go ahead experiment with it and make Vim you own.

Common Commands in Vim

  • :w - Save file
  • :q - Close file
  • :q! - Force close file and revert unsaved changes
  • :wq - Save and close file
  • esc key - Go back from any mode to normal mode
  • i - Enter insert mode
  • o - Next line and insert mode
  • s - delete character and insert
  • dd - delete line

Here is a vim cheat sheet from devhints. You can check it out for more commands.

Some Tips

  • Do not give up on Vim, at least try it for a week or two before deciding to throw it away. I know it's pretty annoying at first but a lot of people end up loving it after using.
  • Do not close Vim, don't exit the terminal without quitting Vim, can corrupt files sometimes
  • Do not use ctrl+z, freezes my Vim for some reason
  • You can use shell commands by prefixing things with !
  • Windows may not have some features that Linux will offer but you can install wsl.
  • Increase your typing speed, it will help you out in general too.
  • Use aliases for speeding up your development process.
  • Practice. That's the best way to get used to Vim.

Some Useful Tools

  • Use a directory jumper, so you don't have to keep cding all the time. I use z
  • You can use a terminal multiplexer for Linux and Mac devices. tmux is a famous one and the only one I know. Never heard of terminal multiplexers on Powershell though

More Resources

  • awesome-neovim - A collection of wonderful neovim plugins & themes for you to try
  • r/vim - A wonderful community if you have any questions regarding Vim
  • r/neovim - Similar to r/vim
  • neovim docs - The official documentation for Neovim
  • vim docs - The documentation for Vim
  • nerd fonts - It lets you have more glyphs and font ligatures

If someone wants to point out something that I missed or any other resource or just has doubts about something I wasn't able to explain properly, please comment it down below.

Hope you liked it!
item.author: aviavinav

item.title: 掌握函数的返回语句 | Mastering the functions' return statement
item.description:
为了结束一个函数，JavaScript 使用语句return 。 这是您将在编码课程中学习的第一件事。

但是你不应该低估它帮助你编写更清晰、性能更好的代码的能力（当你没有很好地掌握它时，反之亦然）。

我在这篇文章中使用 JavaScript 代码来说明我的观点，但它也适用于其他编程语言。

概括

我们使用函数将根据上下文组合在一起的语句组合在一起。 这意味着它们服务于相同的目的，执行相同的任务。

我们将尝试遵循的第一条规则：一个功能=一个责任。 责任不止一项。 它会让你的代码变得更好。

 function addOneTo(a) { const result = a + 1 return result }

函数addOneTo(a)将变量a中包含的值加 1。 我在另一篇文章中详细解释了JavaScript 变量，如果您还不熟悉它们，我强烈建议您阅读它。

换句话说， return语句让我们清楚地向 JavaScript 解释器指示该函数将返回哪个值。 在我们的示例中，我们使用了两个变量。 我们不想返回a ，只返回一个result 。

没有返回语句的函数呢？

那些像我一样拥有算法类的人可能还记得这一点：函数必须总是返回一些东西。 这是一个基本规则。 在您不想返回值的情况下，存在其他东西。 它被称为程序。

问题是：JavaScript 中没有过程。

为了解决这个问题，JavaScript 允许我们返回一个“未定义”的值。 它没有定义。 我们不知道值是什么，所以我们可以说它是空的。

 function prepareObject(obj) { obj.connect({ // ... }) obj.use({ // ... }) return undefined }

这样，您可以像使用过程一样使用函数，将不返回定义的结果/值的可重用语句放在一起。

函数prepareObject运行它的语句，最后我们说函数返回一个undefined的值。 对我们来说，这意味着我们什么都不返回，但是解释器很高兴，因为无论如何你都返回了一个值。

如果您尝试运行prepareObject函数，您会看到它返回值undefined 。

不过，有几种方法可以使代码更简单。 首先，将值留空，如下所示：

 function prepareObject(obj) { obj.connect({ // ... }) obj.use({ // ... }) return }

因为返回了“nothing”，所以 JavaScript 解释器理解您想要返回一个undefined的值并自动执行。

您也可以完全省略该语句，如下所示：

 function prepareObject(obj) { obj.connect({ // ... }) obj.use({ // ... }) }

如果你认为这是一个程序，我明白为什么，但这是错误的。 试试看，运行prepareObject函数，你会看到它返回值undefined 。

前面的代码是有效的。 唯一需要记住的是， return是父函数范围内的“阻塞”语句。

这意味着它表示它所在的功能的停止。 理解这一点很重要，因为这意味着代码位于return语句之后将永远不会被执行。

位于return语句之后的代码就是我们所说的“死代码”。

实际上， return语句的字面意思是：“这个函数运行正常，这是你可以显示给父上下文的结果。 无需进一步阅读。”。

更好地使用return语句

如果您问过自己为什么我们会使用return; 而不是省略该语句，或者如果您想知道为什么我们会在return之后编写代码，这篇文章的这一部分是为您准备的。

一旦你理解了这两个想法，你就有可能更好地使用return语句。 良好地使用return可以提高代码的可读性。

尽快退出职能部门

当我们阅读代码时，无论是我们的还是别人的，数据越多，我们的大脑就越难理解它。 执行上下文之间的跳转是需要考虑的部分数据，每个函数都是一个新的执行上下文。

话虽如此，我们不想使用更少的功能，因为优点多于缺点（可重用性、代码结构等）。

降低代码复杂性的一种方法是减少要考虑的数据数量。 让我们看一个例子。

 function byOrder(a, b) { let result = null if (a.order > b.order) { result = 1 } if (a.order < b.order) { result = -1 } if (a.order == b.order) { result = 0 } return result }

该函数用于告诉JavaScript 函数array.sort如何对数组进行排序。 它是这样使用的： array.sort(byOrder) 。

让我们阅读函数来理解。 让我们看看我们的大脑在调试时会做什么来说明我们的观点。 首先，我看到有一个使用值null初始化的result变量，但是由于我们使用了let语句，所以值会发生变化。 要了解有关此步骤的更多信息，请阅读有关 JavaScript 变量的详细文章。

  • 一个条件：如果a的顺序优于b 。 在我们的例子中，我们将result的值修改为1 。
  • 一个新条件：如果a的阶数小于b 。 这不是我们的情况，无需详细说明这种情况。
  • 一个新条件：如果a的阶数等于b 。 这不是我们这里的情况，不需要详细说明这种情况。
  • 我们看到return语句想要返回result变量的值。 该函数返回1 ，因为我们在第一个条件的范围内。

    • 我们必须阅读（并理解）比必要更多的代码，以确保我们理解这个函数的作用。 上面的第 3 点和第 4 点不是必需的，但我们仍然需要在大脑中执行它们。 我们还必须记住在这些步骤之前发生了什么，才能知道要返回什么。

    现在让我们尝试重写byOrder的代码，让它尽快出来，这要归功于return的特性。 然后我们将做同样的练习来比较代码复杂度。

     function byOrder(a, b) { if (a.order > b.order) { return 1 } if (a.order < b.order) { return -1 } return 0 }

    这个新功能做同样的事情。 它与第一个函数具有相同的签名。 现在让我们尝试阅读它（提醒一下， a.order等于10和b.order等于0 ）：

    1. 首先，我进入函数，马上就有一个条件：如果a的顺序优于b 。 在我们的例子中，我们返回值 1。

    这就是return语句阻塞的方式，它停止执行函数，在我们的特定情况下，其余代码是“死的”。 读它没有意义。 我们不需要了解函数的其余部分来了解它在我们的案例中的作用。

    在阅读过程中，我们的大脑需要考虑的参数要少得多。 它不那么复杂。 这让我想起了 Swift 的guard 。

    几乎在所有情况下都是如此。 尝试为a.order === b.order做练习，即使它是最后测试的条件，第二个函数也不那么复杂。

    使else语句更有用

    另一种改进byOrder代码的方法是使用关键字else和else...if 。

     function byOrder(a, b) { let result = null if (a.order > b.order) { result = 1 } else if (a.order < b.order) { result = -1 } else { result = 0 } return result }

    我们正在通过return来模拟解决方案的行为，因为一旦找到相应的条件，就不会执行下一个条件。

    但是，这个解决方案是不对的，因为我认为它仍然是超载的，并且它比我们上面提到的返回解决方案需要更多的关注。

    有些人通过使用return绕过这个问题。 但是他们忘记了使用else的意义。

     function byOrder(a, b) { if (a.order > b.order) { return 1 } else if (a.order < b.order) { return -1 } else { return 0 } }

    在这种特定情况下，您必须选择： else或return 。 使用return取消了使用else的优点，它也使代码复杂化。 这段代码没有其他目的，只是表明编写者并不真正理解else语句和return语句的微妙之处。 不要那样做。

    通过在此函数中仅使用return ，您删除了一些单词。 但更重要的是，您删除了一个完整的范围（在最后一个else中）并删除了一个缩进级别，正如我们之前看到的，这有助于使您的代码更清晰、更简单。

    就个人而言，我很少在我的代码中使用else ，因为在大多数情况下，我可以编写一个更容易阅读和理解的函数，只使用return 。

    不要让我说我没有说的话， else和else...if语句是非常有用的结构，我仍然根据需要使用它们。 我的观点是它们只在某些情况下有用。 如果有其他关键字或结构最适合我们的情况，那么使用它们是没有用的。 这个建议适用于编程中的一切。

    简而言之，return 语句 ( return ) 允许将一个值（无论是否定义）返回到函数的父上下文。 当 return 语句运行时，它会停止当前函数的执行。

    它有助于优化我们的代码编写，以减少单个函数内的范围数量和不同级别的缩进。 它还有助于防止我们在调试时处理太多数据。



    原文:

    To end a function, JavaScript uses the statement return. The return statement is very simple. It's one of the first thing you'll learn in a coding course.

    But you shouldn't underestimate its capacity to help you write a cleaner and more performing code (and the opposite when you don't master it well enough).

    I am using JavaScript code to illustrate my point in this post, but it also applies to other programming languages.

    Summary

    How does thereturn⁣ statement work?

    A better use of the return statement

    How does the return⁣  statement work?

    We use functions to group together statement which go contextually together. It means they serve the same purpose, do the same task.

    First rule we're going to try and follow : one function = one responsibility. Never more than one responsibility. It will make your code so much better.

    function addOneTo(a) {
    const result = a + 1

    return result
}

    The function addOneTo(a) adds 1 to the value contained in the variable a. I explain JavaScript variables in details in a different post, if you're not comfortable with them yet, I highly encourage you to read it.

    The return statement allows us to return the result of the addition this way : return result.

    In other words, the return statement lets us clearly indicate to the JavaScript interpreter which value this function will return. In our example, we use two variables. We don't want to return a, only result.

    What about functions with no return statement?

    Those of you who, like me, had algorithms classes might remember this : a function must always return something. It's a ground rule. JavaScript follows this rule, functions always return something. In the case where you don't want to return a value, something else exists. It's called procedures.

    The problem is: There are no procedures in JavaScript.

    To solve this problem, JavaScript allows us to return an “undefined” value. It's not defined. We don't know what the value is, so we can say it's empty.

    function prepareObject(obj) {
    obj.connect({
      // ...
    })
    obj.use({
      // ...
    })

    return undefined
}

    This way, you can use functions as you would use procedures, you put together reusable statements that don't return defined results/values.

    The function prepareObject runs its statements, and at the end we say that the function return an undefined value. For us, it means that we return nothing, but the interpreter is happy because you return a value anyway.

    If you try running the prepareObject function, you'll see that it returns the value undefined.

    There are a few ways to make the code simpler, though. First, leave the value blank like this :

    function prepareObject(obj) {
    obj.connect({
      // ...
    })
    obj.use({
      // ...
    })

    return
}

    Because “nothing” is returned, the JavaScript interpreter understands that you want to return an undefined value and does it automatically.

    You can also omit the statement altogether, like this :

    function prepareObject(obj) {
    obj.connect({
      // ...
    })
    obj.use({
      // ...
    })
}

    If you're thinking that is a procedure, I understand why, but it's wrong. Try it, run the prepareObject function, you'll see that it returns the value undefined.

    The return statement is a “blocking” statement

    The fact that return is a classical statement allows us to put it anywhere in the code of a function.

    function doComplicatedStuff(obj) {
    obj.connect({
      // ...
    })

    return obj

    obj.use({
      // ...
    })
}

    The previous code is valid. The only thing you have to remember here is that return is a “blocking” statement in the scope of the parent function.

    Which means that it indicates the stopping of the function in which it is. It's important to understand because it means that the code is located after a return statement will never be executed.

    The code located after a return statement is what we call “dead code”.

    In fact, the return statement literally means : “this function is done running properly, here is the result you can show to the parent context. No need to read further.”.

    A better use of the return statement

    If you have asked yourself why we would use return; instead of omitting the statement, or if you've wondered why we would write code after a return, this part of the post is for you.

    Once you understand these two ideas, it becomes possible for you to better your use of the return statement. A good use of return makes a for a better code readability.

    Getting out of the function as soon as possible

    When we read code, whether it's ours or someone else's, the more data, the harder it is for our brains to understand it. Jumps between execution contexts are part of the data that needs to be considered, and each function is a new execution context.

    This being said, we don't want to use less functions because there are more pros than cons (re-usability, code structuration, etc…).

    A way to reduce the complexity of the code is to reduce the number of data to consider. The return statement can help us do that. Let's see an example.

    function byOrder(a, b) {
    let result = null

    if (a.order > b.order) {
        result = 1
    }

    if (a.order < b.order) {
        result = -1
    }

    if (a.order == b.order) {
        result = 0
    }

    return result
}

    This function is used to tell the JavaScript function array.sort how to sort an array. It's used like this : array.sort(byOrder).

    Let's read the function to understand. Let's see what our brain does when we're debugging to illustrate our point. a.order equals 10 and b.order equals 0.

    1. First, I see that there is a result variable initialized with the value null, but the value is going to change because we use the let statement. To read more about this step, read the detailed post on JavaScript variables.
    2. One condition : if the order of a is superior to b. It is, in our case, we modify the value of result to 1.
    3. A new condition : if the order of a is less than b. It's not our case, no need to go into the details of this condition.
    4. A new condition : if the order of a is equal to b. It's not our case here, no need to go into the details of this condition.
    5. We see that the return statement wants to return the value of the result variable. The function returns 1 because we are in the scope of the first condition.

    We had to read (and understand) more code than necessary to make sure we understood what this function does. The points 3 and 4 above aren't necessary, but we still have to execute them in our brain. We also had to remember what happened before these steps to know what to return.

    Now let's try to rewrite the code of byOrder so it can be out as soon as possible, thanks to the proprieties of return. Then we'll do the same exercise to compare the code complexity.

    function byOrder(a, b) {
    if (a.order > b.order) {
        return 1
    }

    if (a.order < b.order) {
        return -1
    }

    return 0
}

    This new function does the exact same thing. It has the same signature as the first function. Let's try to read it now (as a reminder, a.order equals 10 and b.order equals 0) :

    1. First, I enter the function and there is a condition straight away : if the order of a is superior to b. It is, in our case, so we return the value 1.

    This is how the return statement is blocking, and it stops executing the function, in our specific case, the rest of the code is “dead”. There's no point in reading it. We don't need to understand the rest of the function to understand what it does in our case.

    There are a lot less parameters to take into account for our brain during the reading. It's way less complex. It makes me think about theguard with Swift.

    And it's true in almost every case. Try to do the exercise for a.order === b.order, even if it's the last tested conditions, the second function is less complex.

    Make the else statement more useful

    Another way to better the code of byOrder is to use the keywords else and else...if.

    function byOrder(a, b) {
    let result = null

    if (a.order > b.order) {
        result = 1
    } else if (a.order < b.order) {
        result = -1
    } else {
        result = 0
    }

    return result
}

    We're mimicking the behavior of the solution with the return because the next conditions aren't executed as soon as a corresponding condition is found.

    However, the solution isn't right because I think it's still overloaded, and it requires more focus than the return solution we mentioned above.

    Some bypass this problem by using return. But they forget the point of usingelse.

    function byOrder(a, b) {
    if (a.order > b.order) {
        return 1
    } else if (a.order < b.order) {
        return -1
    } else {
        return 0
    }
}

    In this specific case, you have to choose : either else, or return. The use of return cancels the pros to use else and it complicates the code too. This code has no other purpose than to show that the person who wrote didn't really understand the subtleties of else statement and return statement. Don't do that.

    By using solely the return in this function, you delete a few words. But more importantly, you delete a full scope (in the last else) and you delete a level of indentation, which, as we saw before, help to make your code clearer and less complex.

    Personally, I rarely use else anymore in my code because I can, in most cases, write a function that is easier to read and understand by using return exclusively.

    Don't make me say what I didn't say, else and else...if statements are very useful structures, I still use them as needed. My point here is that they're only useful in some situation. There's no use using them if there are other keywords or structures that are best fitted for our situation. This advice works for everything in programming.

    In short, the return statement (return) allows returning a value, defined or not, to the parent context of the function. When the return statement is run, it stops the execution of the current function.

    It helps to optimize our code writing to reduce the number of scopes and different levels of indentation inside a single function. It also helps prevent having too many data to go through when we're debugging.
item.author: mindsers

item.title: 您知道您可以查看移动网站的 chrome devtool 吗？ | Did you know you can view chrome devtool for a mobile site?
item.description:
使用 devtool，开发人员可以查看控制台日志、错误消息、本地存储、会话存储以及查看网站的源代码。

您可以通过按 F12 或右键单击网页并单击“检查”来访问这些工具。 但是您如何在移动设备上查看它们？

如何在移动设备上访问 devtools？

您无法直接在移动设备上查看开发工具，但您可以通过 USB 将设备连接到 PC 并查看打开的每个浏览器选项卡的开发工具。

那么你是怎么做到的呢？

启用开发者模式

首先让我们在你的安卓设备上启用开发者模式。 这是通过打开手机设置并搜索“内部版本号”来完成的。 找到后，点击内部版本号数次。
开发者模式已启用。 现在在您的设置中搜索“usb 调试”并启用。 你现在已经设置了你的开发者模式。

连接到您的电脑

启用开发者模式后，使用 USB 将手机连接到 PC。

打开 Chrome 开发工具

在您的 PC 上打开 chrome 并导航到chrome://inspect 。 您将在此处看到已连接的设备，并在您的移动设备上打开所有 chrome 选项卡。

如果您右键单击选项卡并单击inspect tab ，您现在可以访问所有开​​发人员工具，就像您在移动设备上查看网站一样。

是不是很厉害！！



原文:

Chrome devtools is a great resource for web developers. With devtool, developers can view console logs, errors messages, local storage, session storage and view the source code of a website.

You can access these tools by pressing F12 or right clicking on a web page and clicking 'inspect'. But how can you view them for mobile?

How can I access devtools on mobile?

You can't view devtools directly on mobile but you can connect the device to a PC via USB and see dev tools for each browser tab open.

So how do you do that?

Enable developer mode

First let's enable developer mode on your android device. This is done by opening up your phone settings and search "Build number". Once you find it, tap the build number several times.
Developer mode has been enabled. Now search for "usb debugging" in your settings and enable. You have now set up your developer mode.

Connect to your PC

With developer mode enabled, connect your phone to your PC with a USB.

Open up Chrome dev tools

Open up chrome on your PC and navigate to chrome://inspect. You will see the connected devices here and all the chrome tabs open on your mobile device.

If you right click on the tab and click inspect tab you now have access to all the developer tools as if you are viewing the site on mobile.

Isn't that awesome!!
item.author: lukeecart

item.title: Python 调度程序 | Python Scheduler
item.description:
用于使用不同类型的模式和语法定期运行函数。 通常，如果必须在特定时间差之后每次都执行任务，则可以使用调度程序。

假设您有一个需要在每天上午 10 点生成的报告，可以使用预定程序触发此过程。

该库的官方文档位于https://schedule.readthedocs.io/en/stable/

要使用此库，您需要使用

现在您已准备好安排作业。

让我们尝试一个简单的例子

import schedule import time # This method is the actual processing that we want to accomplish # with every scheduled execution def job(): print("Job ran") # This defines the frequency to run the program schedule.every().hour.do(job) # while loop to keep the program running in the memory while True: # it will check if any execution is pending, # if yes it will start execution schedule.run_pending() # program will sleep for 1 second and then will again start while loop time.sleep(1)

上述程序将每小时运行一次并打印“Job ran”行。

让我们看一些更多的例子来安排作业语法

有频率的计划工作

# Run job every 15 seconds schedule.every(15).seconds.do(job) # Run job every 15 minutes schedule.every(32).minutes.do(job) # Run job every 12 hours schedule.every(12).hours.do(job) # Run job every 5 days schedule.every(5).days.do(job) # Run job every 2 weeks schedule.every(2).weeks.do(job)

为特定时间安排的工作

# Run job every minute at the 45th second schedule.every().minute.at(":45").do(job) # Run job every hour at the 10th minute schedule.every().hour.at(":10").do(job) # Run job every day at specific time # Everyday at 11 Hour 11 Minutes schedule.every().day.at("11:11").do(job) # Everyday at 11 Hour 11 Minutes 11 Second schedule.every().day.at("11:11:11").do(job)

在以下位置查看工作演示
https://replit.com/@AshutoshSharm35/Python-Scheduler-Demo#main.py https://replit.com/@AshutoshSharm35/Python-Scheduler-Demo#main.py



原文:

is used to run the function periodically using different types of patterns and syntaxes. Normally, if a task has to be performed every time after a specific time difference, a scheduler can be used.

Suppose you have a report which needs to be generated at 10 AM daily, this process can be triggered using a scheduled program.

Python has a “schedule” library that can help you perform this scheduling using very friendly syntaxes.

Official documentation of this library is available at https://schedule.readthedocs.io/en/stable/

To use this library you will need to install it using

Now you are ready to schedule the jobs.

Let's try a simple example

import schedule
import time

# This method is the actual processing that we want to accomplish
# with every scheduled execution
def job():
    print("Job ran")

# This defines the frequency to run the program
schedule.every().hour.do(job)

# while loop to keep the program running in the memory
while True:
        # it will check if any execution is pending, 
        # if yes it will start execution
    schedule.run_pending()

        # program will sleep for 1 second and then will again start while loop
    time.sleep(1)

The above program will run every hour and print the line “Job ran”.

Let's look at some more examples to schedule job syntaxes

Scheudled job with frequency

# Run job every 15 seconds
schedule.every(15).seconds.do(job)

# Run job every 15 minutes
schedule.every(32).minutes.do(job)

# Run job every 12 hours
schedule.every(12).hours.do(job)

# Run job every 5 days
schedule.every(5).days.do(job)

# Run job every 2 weeks
schedule.every(2).weeks.do(job)

Scheduled job for specific time

# Run job every minute at the 45th second
schedule.every().minute.at(":45").do(job)

# Run job every hour at the 10th minute
schedule.every().hour.at(":10").do(job)

# Run job every day at specific time

# Everyday at 11 Hour 11 Minutes
schedule.every().day.at("11:11").do(job)

# Everyday at 11 Hour 11 Minutes 11 Second
schedule.every().day.at("11:11:11").do(job)

Checkout the working demo at
https://replit.com/@AshutoshSharm35/Python-Scheduler-Demo#main.py
item.author: ashusharmatech

item.title: 你需要知道的5件事<Input>标签 | 5 things you need to know about <Input> tag
item.description:
嗨，伙计们，你们还好吗？ 我希望你做得很好。 在这个博客中，我将分享一些关于<input>标记的属性，我希望我早点知道这些属性。

一、重点：

假设我们的<input>标签中没有边框，我们可以使用以下方法实现：

但是，当我们开始在输入标签中输入内容时，我们会再次获得这些边框。 所以，这个问题的一个简单解决方案是：

 input :focus { outline : none ; }

自动完成帮助我们完成输入字段，但有时它会变得烦人并提示错误的细节。 关闭自动完成的方法是：

反应：

 <input type= ”text” autoComplete= "new-off" >

有时，文本内容不是我们想要发送的，它可能是图像、gif、视频等。我们可以使用type="file"属性发送这种类型的数据。

通常，我们使用e.target.value来访问输入值，但在这里我们可以使用e.target.files[0]来访问文件数据。 它以对象的形式包含文件的详细信息，例如名称、路径等。

示例：在编辑/制作博客时，当我们登陆编辑博客页面时，我们希望输入成为焦点。

 <input type= "text" autofocus >

希望用户从特定的选项列表中进行选择？ 我们可以使用列表属性。 让我们看一个例子。

 <form onSubmit= {function} > <input list= "anime" name= "anime" > <datalist id= "anime" > <option value= "Naruto" > <option value= "My Hero Academia" > <option value= "Death Note" > <option value= "Dragon Ball Z" > <option value= "Attack on Titan" > </datalist> <input type= "submit" > </form>

就是这样，伙计们，我希望你喜欢这些技巧，并将这些小技巧融入你的下一个项目:)

如果您知道其他提示/技巧，请在评论中告诉我。 谢谢阅读 ：）

与我联系 -



原文:

Hi folks, how are you holding up? I hope you’re doing good. In this blog, I’m gonna share some properties about the <input> tag that I wish I’d known sooner.

1. Focus:

The :focus selector comes into action when we click the input element and we can type content in the input field.

Suppose we don’t have a border in our <input> tag, we can implement this using:

But, when we begin to type content in our input label, we get those borders back again. So, a simple solution to this problem will be:

input:focus{ outline: none;
}

2. Autocomplete:

Autocomplete helps us to complete input fields, but sometimes it becomes irritating and suggests wrong details. A method to turn off autocomplete would be:

React:

<input type=”text” autoComplete="new-off">

HTML:

<input type=”text” auto-complete="off">

3. File input:

Sometimes, text content is not what we want to send, it might be images, gifs, videos, etc. We can send this type of data using the type="file" attribute.

Usually, we use e.target.value to access the input value, but here we can access the file data using e.target.files[0]. It contains the details of the file like its name, path, etc in form of an object.

The "no file chosen" text is black by default, you can change this to any color using input[type='file'].

input[type='file'] { color: rgb(255, 255, 255)
}

4. Autofocus

The input autofocus attribute specifies that an input field should automatically get focus when the page loads. Example: While editing/making blogs, when we land on the edit blog page, we would want the input to be in focus.

<input type="text" autofocus>

5. Input list attribute

Want the user to choose from a specific list of options? We can use the list attribute. Let’s look at an example.

<form onSubmit={function}> <input list="anime" name="anime"> <datalist id="anime"> <option value="Naruto"> <option value="My Hero Academia"> <option value="Death Note"> <option value="Dragon Ball Z"> <option value="Attack on Titan"> </datalist> <input type="submit" >
</form>

That’s it, folks, I hope you enjoyed these tips and would incorporate these small techniques in your next projects :)

If you know of other tips/tricks, let me know in the comments. Thanks for reading :)

Connect with me on -

item.description:

有了这个扩展，您可以使用 html 文件中的 CSS 类搜索相关的 CSS 文件，并在那里自己编辑 CSS 文件！ 以下是它的工作原理：

所有这些信息都与您的光标当前所在的行内联。

您还可以将鼠标悬停在消息上以获取有关特定代码行的更多信息！

这些是 5 个强大、有用的 VSCode 扩展，可以提高您的工作效率和工作流程！

签字。

推特| 领英



原文:

Here are the 5 most useful Visual Studio Code extensions to improve your workflow!

  • Error Lens
  • CSS Peek
  • GitLens
  • Import Cost
  • Version Lens

1. Error Lens

Error Lens is an extension which allows you to see error, warning and diagnostic messages in line with your code without having to hover or click on anything! It also highlights the line with different colors to help you better visualize and differentiate between errors, warnings and other messages!

2. CSS Peek

With this extension, you can search for relevant CSS files by using CSS classes in html file and edit CSS files there itself! Here's how it works:

3. GitLens

GitLens is a powerful extension which can display when a particular line was committed, by whom it was committed and in which pull request it was included along with the commit message! All of this information is inline with the line upon which your cursor is currently located.

You can also hover over the message to get even more information about a particular line of code!

There are many more features in GitLens which you can explore and play around with!

4. Import Cost

Import Cost allows us to see the size of the imported package inline in the editor itself!

5. Version Lens

Version Lens fetches information about dependency versions and displays it on top of the package!

These were the 5 VSCode extensions which are powerful, useful and can improve your productivity and workflow!

Signing off.

item.title: 小企业面临的 5 大挑战 | 5 Major Challenges Facing Small Enterprises
item.description:
南迪尼班达里

发表于对于大多数人来说，拥有和经营一家企业的前景令人恐惧，尤其是当您考虑到在此过程中会遇到多少障碍时。 有一个概念有时是最简单的部分。 最困难和最可怕的方面是开始并在此过程中很好地管理它。 贵公司的前两年至关重要，大多数小企业都失败了。 那些敢于这样做的人必须处理业务的各个方面，包括工人、营销、行政、会计，并且可能最关键的是，确保不丢失任何线索。

如果您以创业为专业并需要创业分配帮助，那么您可以随时与我们联系。

现金流问题

有充分的理由，财务挑战通常是企业家最害怕的挑战。 为了支持您的启动，您必须管理几个传入和传出事务。 同时，您必须在按时完成所有工作。 该方法是记录每笔交易，创建基本预测并坚持下去。 不要解雇它。 这种类型的会计可能会拯救您的公司。 聘请会计师是最好和最明显的解决方案，但由于您才刚刚起步，您自然负担不起。 但别担心，还有其他选择。

随着技术的进步，有多种软件替代方案可用于监控付款、开具发票、管理现金流等。 当付款到期时，该程序会通知您、更新您的银行对账单并提交您的增值税。 您还可以使用商业方法，例如每次收到订单时要求预付定金。 此外，要求您的客户尽快支付发票通常是值得的。 尝试说服您的商家延长您的付款期限。 您可以通过正确的计划克服现金流挑战。

防止倦怠

如果您经营一家小型企业，您很可能希望自己处理所有事情。 您对自己的产品或服务了如指掌，并且可以比其他任何人更快、更好地做到这一点。 但这几乎肯定会让你付出代价。 你的健康和家庭关系，而不是你的钱。 倦怠和压力往往会导致你放弃一切，而你不想这样做。 为避免这种情况，最好学会委派你的工作，甚至可以聘请一些帮助，无论是全职还是每天一小时。

让他们协助您完成耗费您大量时间的简单任务，以便您可以专注于组织中更重要的方面。 委派时，记得清楚地说明你的职责。 沟通对于富有成效的工作关系至关重要。 你不必做所有事情，记住你并没有放弃控制； 你只是得到帮助。

获取和留住客户

正如您肯定知道的那样，吸引客户的关键是提供与众不同且能够满足他们需求的产品。 因此，进行一些市场调查，确定您的目标受众，确定他们的痛点，并确定如何缓解它们。
营销对您的业务也很重要。 保持简单，并选择一个突出的媒体渠道来宣传您的业务。 让您的评论简洁而难忘，因为头脑定位是成功的一半。 您还应该尽可能地有空，以便您可以立即或在几个小时内做出回应。 让这些电子邮件变得风度翩翩，而不是使用自动回复，并相信人们会注意到其中的差异。 您可能总是向特定的潜在客户提供演示服务，您认为您可以在其中赚到很多钱，让他们体验与您合作的感觉。

过度依赖一位客户

可以理解的是，即使是一位优秀的、重要的、准时付费的客户对初创公司来说也是个好消息，但这也可能是一个令人头疼的问题。 也就是说，它不会像承包商那样使您成为企业主。 将您的重点从单个客户转移并尝试找到可能不那么可靠的新客户可能很困难，但这很重要。
可能会出现各种问题，甚至大客户也可能不再需要您的产品。 那时，如果大客户停止付款，您就需要额外的普通客户来维持您的公司的运转。

增长与质量

总有一天，您的业务将以如此快速的速度发展，以至于您将无法单独关注每个客户。 结果，您会注意到质量开始恶化。 这是您必须就是否雇用或外包服务做出关键决定的时候。 通常，首先使您的业务成功的是个人风格。 但不要绝望； 并非一切都消失了。 这是一个艰难的决定，但并不一定意味着比赛结束。 两者都是可能的。

结论

小型企业是私人持有的，与公司或常规规模的公司相比，员工人数和年收入更少。 就政府援助资格和税收优惠待遇而言，“小”的概念因国家和行业而异。

克里斯蒂安·普罗奇诺 - May 27 5月27日



原文:

nandini bhandari

Posted on May 28

For most individuals, the prospect of owning and running a business is terrifying, especially when you consider how many obstacles you can encounter along the way. Having a concept is sometimes the easiest part. The most difficult and frightening aspect is getting started and managing it well along the way. The first two years of your firm are critical, and most small businesses fail. Those that are daring enough to do so must handle all aspects of the business, including workers, marketing, administration, accounting, and, probably most crucially, ensuring that no leads are lost.

If you are pursuing entrepreneurship as your major and need entrepreneurship assignment help then you can contact us anytime.

Cash Flow Issues

For good reason, financial challenges are frequently the ones that entrepreneurs are most terrified of. There are several incoming and outgoing transactions that you must manage in order to support your startup. At the same time, you must manage everything while meeting deadlines. The approach is to log every transaction, create a basic prediction, and stick to it. Don't dismiss it. This type of accounting might save your company. Hiring an accountant is the best and most obvious solution, but because you are just starting out, it is natural that you cannot afford it. But don't worry, there are alternatives.

With the advancement of technology, there are several software alternatives for monitoring payments, issuing invoices, managing cash flow, and so on. When a payment is due, the program will notify you, update your bank statements, and submit your VAT. You may also use business methods, such as asking for a down payment every time you get an order. Furthermore, it is usually worthwhile to request that your clients pay their invoices as soon as possible. Try to persuade your merchants to extend your payment deadline. You can overcome cash flow challenges with the correct plan.

Preventing Burnout

If you operate a small business, you most likely want to handle everything yourself. You know your product or service through and out, and you can do it faster and better than anybody else. But it will almost certainly cost you. Your health and family ties, not your money. Burnout and stress may often cause you to give up on everything, which you do not want to do. To avoid this, it is better to learn to delegate your work and perhaps even employ some help, whether full time or simply an hour a day.

Allow them to assist you with the easy tasks that consume a lot of your time so that you can concentrate on the more essential aspects of your organisation. When delegating, remember to state your duties clearly. Communication is essential for a productive working relationship. You don't have to do everything, and remember that you're not giving up control; you're simply getting assistance.

Getting And Keeping Customers

As you are surely aware, the key to attracting clients is to offer a product that is distinctive and can meet their demands. So, conduct some market research, identify your target audience, determine their pain areas, and determine how you can alleviate them.
Marketing is also important for your business. Keep it simple and choose a prominent media channel to publicise your business. Make your comment succinct yet memorable for your buyer, because mind positioning is half the battle. You should also be available as much as possible so that you can respond immediately or within a few hours. Make those emails personable instead of using automated answers, and believe that people will notice the difference. You may always provide a demo service to particular potential clients where you believe you can earn a lot of money to give them a taste of what working with you would be like.

Over Relying On One Customer

Understandably, even one nice, significant, on-time paying customer is fantastic news for a startup company, but it may also be a headache. That is, it does not make you a business owner as much as it does a contractor. It might be tough to shift your emphasis away from a single customer and try to locate new ones that may not be as dependable, but it is vital.
All sorts of issues might arise, and even a large client may cease to want your product. That's when you need additional modest clients to keep your firm going if the large one stops paying.

Growth vs. Quality

There will come a moment when your business will be developing at such a rapid speed that you will be unable to focus on each customer individually. As a result, you will notice that the quality begins to deteriorate. And this is the point at which you must make a critical decision about whether to hire or outsource services. Typically, it is the personal touch that makes your business successful in the first place. But don't despair; not all is gone. It is a difficult decision, but it does not have to signal the end of the game. Both are possible.

Conclusion

A small business is one that is privately held and has fewer workers and less yearly income than a corporation or a regular-sized firm. The concept of "small" in terms of eligibility for government assistance and favourable tax treatment varies by nation and industry.

Cristian Prochnow - May 27
item.author: nandinibhandari

item.title: 暑假和编码 | Summer Vacation and Coding
item.description:
桑杰·辛格·拉杰普特

发表于夏天到了！！

我希望每个人都完成了他们的考试，现在我们有一个很好的长暑假。

如果我必须在更大范围内对它们进行分类，那么这个暑假你可以做几件事，那么我们有两种不同的方式 DSA 和开发。 大多数学生将尝试在开发或 DSA 领域工作。

对于这篇文章，让我们专注于开发方面，

  • 您可以采取不同的途径来开始您的开发之旅。 它不像 DSA，您可以解决一组已定义的问题。

  • 让我们谈谈可以遵循的最简单的开发路径之一。 为初学者选择一些编程语言，如 HTML、CSS 和 Javascript。

  • 首先，尝试制作一个可以与您的兴趣相关的单页应用程序（SPA），或者您可以制作自己的投资组合网站。

  • 现在下一步将是创建一个您想要获取用户反馈的网站。 就像登录页面或某种调查网站的形式一样。 同样，这取决于您想如何探索网络世界。 为此，您需要更多技术，我的偏好是 PHP 和 MySQL。

  • 您的网站已准备就绪，但看起来仍然很旧，缺乏响应能力并且使用速度很慢。 要解决此问题，您必须使用某些 CSS 框架，例如 TailwindCSS 和 Chakra UI，以及前端框架，例如 ReactJS 和 Angular。

  • 现在，下一步是将表单数据保存在远程数据库中。 您可以使用 Postgres (SQL) 和 MongoDB(NoSQL) 数据库。 要实现这种效果，您必须使用某些框架，例如 Node js、Django 和 React Js。

所以现在你在不同的技术栈的帮助下做了一个非常好的项目。 你可以把这个项目放在你的简历中，并寻找一些与你在同一技术堆栈上工作的初创公司。

快乐学习:)

安瓦尔·萨达特·阿尤布—— May 26 5月26日

Suraj Vishwakarma - Suraj Vishwakarma - May 12 5月12日



原文:

Sanjay Singh Rajpoot

Posted on May 28

It's summer time!!

I hope is everyone is done with their exams, and now we have a good long summer break.

You can do several things this summer break if I have to classify them on a larger scale then we have two different ways DSA and Development. Most of the students will try to work in the Development or DSA domain.

For this post let's focus on the development side,

  • There are different paths that you can take to start your development journey. It's not like DSA where you have a defined set of questions that you can solve.

  • let's talk about one of the simplest paths that one can follow for development. Pick a few programming languages for beginners it would be HTML, CSS and Javascript.

  • First, try to make a single page application(SPA) it can be related to your interest or you can make your own portfolio website.

  • Now next step would be to create a website where you want to take user feedback. Like in the form of a Login page or some sort of survey website. Again it's up to your how you want to explore the web world. For this, you need a few more technologies my preferences would be PHP and MySQL.

  • Your website is ready but it still looks old, lacks responsiveness and is slow to use. To fix this issue you have to use certain CSS frameworks such TailwindCSS, and Chakra UI along with Frontend frameworks such ReactJS, and Angular.

  • Now, the next step would be to save your form data on a remote database. You can use Postgres (SQL), and MongoDB(NoSQL) databases. To make this happen effect you have to use certain frameworks such as Node js, Django, and React Js.

So now you have made a really good project with the help of different tech stacks. You can put this project in your resume and look for some startups which work on the same tech stack as you did.

Happy Learning :)

Anwar Sadat Ayub - May 26

Suraj Vishwakarma - May 12

item.title: 离开组织时不要丢失 Github 贡献 | Don't lose your Github contributions when you leave an organization
item.description:
作为软件工程师，在我们的职业生涯中经常会换工作，现在这是一件好事，因为你得到了大幅加薪💰，加入新组织的兴奋也在那里，也许你也在重新定位（到班加罗尔?) 到一个新的地方。

在这种兴奋中，我们大多数人都忘记了很快你就会被踢出你以前的 Github 组织，在你任职期间你可能已经做了数百甚至数千次提交，一旦你被踢出，所有这些提交都会消失在一个✨ jiffy ✨ 来自你的 Github 个人资料。

如果你是像我这样非常关心他们的 Github 个人资料的人，那么如果你没有做必要的事情将这些贡献保留在你的个人资料中，那么你将深感遗憾（比如这个人）。
软件工程师的 Github 个人资料就像他们的简历，或者至少我将其视为一份，因此保持其最新、真实和相关是很重要的。 在您的 Github 个人资料上拥有一致的贡献图可以帮助您被未来可能的招聘人员注意到，并且总的来说，它显示了您作为软件工程师的一致性。

你可以在这里查看我的 Github 个人资料

现在让我们深入了解它🎯

了解何时显示提交很重要

如果满足以下所有条件，提交将出现在您的贡献图表上：

  1. 用于提交的电子邮件地址与您在 GitHub.com 上的帐户相关联。
  2. 提交是在独立的存储库中进行的，而不是分叉。
  3. 提交是在存储库的默认分支中进行的

此外，以下至少一项必须为真：

  • 您是存储库的协作者，或者是拥有该存储库的组织的成员。
  • 您已经分叉了存储库。
  • 您已在存储库中打开拉取请求或问题。
  • 那么我能做些什么来保持我来之不易的贡献呢？
    1. 为您在组织中贡献的存储库加注星标。 这样，即使您离开拥有存储库的组织或删除存储库的分支，您对这些存储库的提交仍将保留在您的贡献图中。
    2. 但由于大多数时候它是专有代码，因此不建议这样做。

    1. 通过在您的电子邮件设置中删除它来取消验证您的公司电子邮件地址。 然后，您可以重新添加它而无需验证以保持任何关联的提交链接到您的帐户。

    2. 将您的主要电子邮件地址从您的公司电子邮件更改为您的个人电子邮件。

    3. 验证您的新主电子邮件地址。

    还是主演一次就足以保留所有过去的提交？

    然后，您可以重新添加它而无需验证以保持任何关联的提交链接到您的帐户。 参考

    如果此博客对您有所帮助，请与其他工程师分享，我不希望任何工程师失去他们的贡献 :)



原文:

As Software engineers there are often times in our careers when we change jobs, now this is a good thing since you get a significant raise 💰, the excitement of joining a new organization is also there and maybe you are also re-locating (to Bangalore?) to a new place.

In this excitement, most of us forget that soon you will be kicked out of your previous Github organization, where you might have made hundreds if not thousands of commits during your tenure and once you are kicked out, all of these commits will disappear in a ✨ jiffy ✨ from you Github profile.

If you are someone like me who cares deeply about their Github profile, then you will be in deep regret (like this person) if you don't do the needful to retain those contributions on your profile.
A Github profile for software engineers is like their resume or at least I treat it like one, hence it's important to keep it up-to-date, authentic and relevant. Having a consistent contributions graph on your Github profile helps you get noticed by possible future recruiters and in general, it shows your consistency as a software engineer.

You can checkout my Github profile here

Now let's get into the meat of it 🎯

It is important to understand when commits are shown

Commits will appear on your contributions graph if they meet all of the following conditions:

  1. The email address used for the commits is associated with your account on GitHub.com.
  2. The commits were made in a standalone repository, not a fork.
  3. The commits were made in the repository's default branch

In addition, at least one of the following must be true:

  • You are a collaborator on the repository or are a member of the organization that owns the repository.
  • You have forked the repository.
  • You have opened a pull request or issue in the repository.
  • ✨ You have starred the repository. ✨

So what can I do to keep my hard-earned contributions?

  1. Star the repositories you are contributing to in your organization. That way, your commits to those repositories will remain in your contributions graph even if you leave the organization that owns the repository or delete your fork of the repository.
  2. [Not recommended / Not ethical] If you still have the codebase locally, you can reinitialize the repository and upload it as a new private repository to your account. But since most of the times it is proprietory code, hence this is not recommended.

  1. Unverify your company email address by deleting it in your Email settings. You can then re-add it without verifying to keep any associated commits linked to your account.

  2. Change your primary email address from your company email to your personal email.

  3. Verify your new primary email address.

1) Regarding the advice to star the repos before leaving, does this mean that only commits post-starring will remain? Or is starring once sufficient to retain all past commits?

Ans - all commits will remain, so star the repositories as soon as you can just in case.

2) What if commits were made using a company email?

Ans - Unverify your company email address by deleting it in your Email settings. You can then re-add it without verifying to keep any associated commits linked to your account. ( refer )

References

If this blog helped you then please share it with other engineers out there, I don't want any engineer to lose their contributions ever :)

item.title: 了解带有标志的`rails new`之间的区别 | Know the difference between `rails new` with flags
item.description:
每当我做rails new时， --skip-xxx和--skip-xxx有什么区别？ 我想知道每次我做rails new时--skip-xxx和不是--skip-xxx有什么区别。

我做了什么

  • 在这个存储库中，使用Rails version和flag组合运行rails new的代码输出可以被 git 标签引用。

    标签的名称格式为{Rails version}-{flag} 。

    例如，带有--minimal的 Rails 7.0.1 代码输出可以由标记7.0.1-minimal引用。

    目前支持的 Rails 版本如下

    用法

    当您看到它时可能很明显，但以防万一，这里是如何使用它。

    例如，在 Rails 7.0.1 中，有一个名为--api的标志，您想了解指定它如何更改生成的代码。

    旗帜从上面我们可以看出，指定--api会在输出结果中产生以下差异。

    • 通过添加config.api_only = true设置将中间件配置更改为所需的最小值。
    • 等等...

    您还可以通过编写自己的 URL 来比较任意标签。 有关如何在 GitHub 上比较标签的更多信息，请参阅以下文档。

    • Comparing commits - GitHub Docs 比较提交 - GitHub Docs


原文:

Whenever I do rails new, what is the difference between --skip-xxx and --skip-xxx? I was wondering what the difference is between --skip-xxx and not --skip-xxx every time I do rails new.

What I made

  • https://github.com/snaka/rails_new_flags

In this repository, the code output from running rails new with the Rails version and flag combination can be referenced by git tags.

The name of the tag is in the form {Rails version}-{flag}.

For example, Rails 7.0.1 code output with --minimal can be referenced by the tag 7.0.1-minimal.

Currently, the supported Rails versions are as follows

Usage

It may be obvious when you see it, but just in case, here is how to use it.

For example, in Rails 7.0.1, there is a flag called --api and you want to find out how specifying it changes the generated code.

Table in README.markdown(Following is an excerpt) to the --api flag line, click on the 🔍 at the 7.0.1 position to see the diff on GitHub.

flag 6.0.4.4 6.1.4.4 7.0.1
--api 🔍 🔍 🔍

(Diff. display as of 2022-01-08)

From the above, we can see that specifying --api makes the following difference in the output results.

  • Gemfile に sprockets-rails, importmap-rails, turbo-rails, stimulus-rails, jbuilder, ... (Omitted) ... is removed
  • Parent class of ApplicationController changes from ActionController::Base to ActionController::API.
  • Middleware configuration changes to the minimum required by adding config.api_only = true setting.
  • And so on...

You can also compare arbitrary Tags by writing your own URLs. For more information on how to compare tags on GitHub, please refer to the following document.

  • Comparing commits - GitHub Docs

item.title: 使用 Docker Compose 部署 MariaDB 服务后运行服务 | To Run Services After Deploying The MariaDB Service Using Docker Compose
item.description:
橿原明

发表于但是，在部署依赖服务后，它无法控制服务运行的顺序，如下图的NG案例。 例如，在使用 MariaDB（与 MySQL 类似的数据库）的情况下，这意味着 Docker 运行任何服务而无需等待完成初始化 MariaDB，例如，如果您只设置了depends_on键就可以了。 本文以部署 MariaDB 服务后运行 Node.js 服务的方式为例，以使用 Docker Compose 部署依赖服务后运行服务为例。

下图是仅使用depends_on键和本文方法运行服务顺序的对比。

源代码

这个示例源代码是根据snowcait在这篇原创文章的讨论中告诉我的源代码（用日语写的）制作的。 我感谢 snowcait 给了我这个想法。
作为重要说明，如果您需要 Docker 等待完成以在/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d中运行脚本，则应将/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d中初始化的用户名和密码输入到healthcheck键中的 test 命令中。 /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d在运行 Node.js 之前。 如果您只创建一些表而不创建其他用户，最好让代码访问在/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d脚本中创建的新表。

码头工人-compose.yml

 version : ' 3' services : mariadb : image : mariadb:10.7 container_name : sandbag_mariadb environment : - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=${MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD} - MARIADB_DATABASE=${MARIADB_DATABASE} - MARIADB_USER=${MARIADB_WEB_USER} - MARIADB_PASSWORD=${MARIADB_WEB_PASSWORD} - MARIADB_API_USER=${MARIADB_API_USER} - MARIADB_API_PASSWORD=${MARIADB_API_PASSWORD} - MARIADB_HOST=% tty : true volumes : - ./sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d networks : - datastream healthcheck : test : [ " CMD" , " mariadb" , " -u${MARIADB_API_USER}" , " -p${MARIADB_API_PASSWORD}" , " -e" , " use ${MARIADB_DATABASE}" ] interval : 10s timeout : 5s retries : 5 start_period : 5s web : env_file : - .env build : context : ./web/. dockerfile : " Dockerfile" ports : - ${WEB_PORT}:${WEB_PORT} depends_on : mariadb : condition : service_healthy command : [ " npm" , " test" ] volumes : - ./volume/log/web:/usr/src/logger/web networks : - datastream networks : datastream :

源码执行结果

您可以在以下 URL 中查看此源代码的执行结果。

致谢

正如我之前指出的， snowcait在这篇原始文章的讨论中为我提供了大部分源代码（用日语编写） 。 我感谢 snowcait 给了我这个想法。

这篇原创文章是我写的以下内容。 这是这篇原始文章的一部分从日语到英语的翻译。

Ajeet Singh Raina - Ajeet Singh Raina - Mar 25 3月25日



原文:

Akira Kashihara

Posted on May 28

The depends_on key in docker-compose.yml can set the order to start running each service such as starting the Node.js service after starting the MariaDB service. However, it cannot control the order to run services after deploying the dependent service as shown the NG case in the figure below. For example, in the case of using MariaDB(a similar database to MySQL), it means that Docker runs any services without waiting to finish initializing MariaDB such as making tables if you just set the depends_on keys only. This article shows the way to run the Node.js service after deploying the MariaDB service as examples to run services after deploying the dependent service using Docker Compose.

The following figure indicates the comparison of running services order between only using the depends_on key and this article method.

Source Code

This sample source code was made based on the source code that snowcait told me in the Discussion of this original article (written in Japanese). I thank snowcait to give me this idea.
As important notes, you should type the user name and password that are initialized in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d to the test command in the healthcheck key if you need Docker to wait for the finish to run the script in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d before running Node.js. It is better to make the code to access a new table that was made in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d script if you only make some tables without making other users.

.github/workflows/test.yml 

name: Actions Test on: push: branches: - dev-* jobs: sandbag_test: runs-on: ubuntu-20.04 timeout-minutes: 5 env: MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${{secrets.MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD}} MARIADB_DATABASE: ${{secrets.MARIADB_DATABASE}} MARIADB_WEB_USER: ${{secrets.MARIADB_WEB_USER}} MARIADB_WEB_PASSWORD: ${{secrets.MARIADB_WEB_PASSWORD}} MARIADB_API_USER: ${{secrets.MARIADB_API_USER}} MARIADB_API_PASSWORD: ${{secrets.MARIADB_API_PASSWORD}} MARIADB_PORT: ${{secrets.MARIADB_PORT}} DB_HOST: ${{secrets.DB_HOST}} WEB_PORT: ${{secrets.WEB_PORT}} steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v2 - name: Shutdown MariaDB run: sudo service mysql stop - name: Run docker-compose shell: bash run: | touch .env docker-compose -v docker-compose up --build --abort-on-container-exit working-directory: ./

docker-compose.yml 

version: '3'
services: mariadb: image: mariadb:10.7 container_name: sandbag_mariadb environment: - MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD=${MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD} - MARIADB_DATABASE=${MARIADB_DATABASE} - MARIADB_USER=${MARIADB_WEB_USER} - MARIADB_PASSWORD=${MARIADB_WEB_PASSWORD} - MARIADB_API_USER=${MARIADB_API_USER} - MARIADB_API_PASSWORD=${MARIADB_API_PASSWORD} - MARIADB_HOST=% tty: true volumes: - ./sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d networks: - datastream healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "mariadb", "-u${MARIADB_API_USER}", "-p${MARIADB_API_PASSWORD}", "-e", "use ${MARIADB_DATABASE}"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 5s web: env_file: - .env build: context: ./web/. dockerfile: "Dockerfile" ports: - ${WEB_PORT}:${WEB_PORT} depends_on: mariadb: condition: service_healthy command: ["npm", "test"] volumes: - ./volume/log/web:/usr/src/logger/web networks: - datastream
networks: datastream:

The Execution Result of The Source Code

You can check the execution result of this source code in the following URL.

https://github.com/KASHIHARAAkira/actions-playground/runs/6634973345?check_suite_focus=true

Acknowledgement

As I indicated before, snowcait provided me the most of this source code in the Discussion of this original article (written in Japanese). I thank snowcait to give me this idea.

This original article is the following that is written by me. This is a translation of a portion of this original article from Japanese to English.

Ajeet Singh Raina - Mar 25

item.title: 距离 CodeLand 2022 不到三周 🤯 | CodeLand 2022 is less than three weeks away 🤯
item.description:
你好，亲爱的社区！

您可能知道， CodeLand 。 我们非常兴奋地为您带来为期两天的虚拟灵感、动力和学习！

多年来，CodeLand 取得了长足的进步，其历史上的每一章都经过精心设计，以满足当时不断发展的社区的需求。 对于 2022 年，您将在该计划中看到特别关注 Web 可访问性——因为这正是 CodeNewbie 和 DEV 社区所要求的！ 我们的目标是取悦🌈

门票 0 美元起

今年的 CodeLand 有很多令人兴奋的事情，以至于很难选择“最令人兴奋”的功能，但我们一直听到的一件特别棒的事情是，该活动将再次按你的意愿付费——起价为 0 美元. 我们认为，为刚开始职业生涯的全球观众提供一个以各种方式都可以访问的会议非常重要。 处于那个阶段可能意味着资金通常很紧张，我们希望在您所在的地方与大家见面。

惊人的扬声器

如果没有业内知名人士和我们鼓舞人心的社区成员的精心策划的演讲，CodeLand 将一事无成。

今年，您将听到主旨演讲人Angie Jones （倡导各级开发人员和 IBM Master Inventor）、 Kelsey Hightower （有影响力的云计算和开源领导者）和Tracy Chou （在线安全的先驱和倡导者）和反骚扰工具，更不用说时代杂志 2022 年度女性奖得主了）。 您还将听到来自 CodeNewbie 社区的 15 位出色的个人，他们有相关的故事和指导与您分享。

注册加入我们

为了加入 CodeLand 并获得完整的虚拟体验、有资格获得奖品和赠品并参与活动，您需要注册。

我们期待在我们的“嘉宾名单”上看到您的名字，并期待您出席今年的活动💜



原文:

Cross-Posted from CodeNewbie Community.

Hello, dear community!

As you might know, CodeLand. -our annual conference for early-career software developers (and their champions)- is coming up on June 16 & 17, 2022. We couldn't be more excited to bring you what's sure to be two days of virtual inspiration, motivation, and learning!

Over the years, CodeLand has evolved a great deal and each chapter in its history was carefully designed to fit the needs of our growing community at the time. For 2022, you'll see in the program that there's a particular focus on web accessibility — because that's what the CodeNewbie and DEV communities asked for! We aim to please 🌈

Tickets start at $0

There's so much to get excited about at CodeLand this year that it's hard to pick a "most exciting" feature, but one thing we keep hearing is particularly awesome is that the event will be pay-what-you-want again — starting at $0. We believe it's important to deliver a conference that's accessible in every way for our global audience of folks just starting out in their careers. Being at that stage can mean that money is often tight, and we want to meet you all where you're at.

Amazing speakers

CodeLand would be nothing without our highly curated talks from prominent figures in the industry and our inspiring community members alike.

This year, you'll get to hear from keynoters Angie Jones (advocate for developers at every level and IBM Master Inventor), Kelsey Hightower (influential cloud computing and open source leader), and Tracy Chou (a pioneer for and advocate of online safety and anti-harassment tooling, not to mention a Time Magazine 2022 Women of the Year award winner). You'll also hear from 15 amazing individuals from the CodeNewbie Community who have relevant stories and guidance to share with you.

>> Browse the CodeLand Program

Register to join us

In order to join us at CodeLand and get the full virtual experience, be eligible for prizes and giveaways, and participate in activities, you'll need to register.

>> Register for CodeLand

We look forward to seeing your name on our "guest list" and your presence at this year's event 💜

item.title: 在 Windows 中设置 Tailwind CSS | Setting up Tailwind CSS in Windows
item.description:
首先，

什么是顺风 CSS？

与其他框架不同，它没有预先构建类以添加到 HTML 标记中。

相反，它使用了不同的方法。 它通过使用基于实用程序的类带来了低得多的控制级别。

您只需将微小的组件组​​合在一起就可以构建一个独特的 UI。

它对创建响应式复杂设计非常有帮助。

在它的官方网站上查看更多信息

安装

脚步 -

  • 确保您的系统中安装了 NodeJ。 如果没有在这里下载。

  • 在要使用/安装它的文件夹的特定位置打开的终端中写入以下命令-

 npm init - y npm install - D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer vite npx tailwindcss init - p
  • 创建一个 CSS 文件并将其链接到您的 HTML 文件并使用以下代码对其进行编辑-
 @tailwind base ; @tailwind components ; @tailwind utilities ;

  • 在“tailwind.config.js”文件中，将“content:[]”替换为“content:[”*”]”

  • 在“package.json”中添加/编辑以下 JSON 行。

 "scripts" : { "start" : "vite" ;
}
  • 现在，最后在终端中编写以下代码来启动开发服务器以运行 Tailwind CSS-

现在去哪里？

现在您已准备好进入 Tailwind CSS。 我为大家找到了一些很棒的资源（2022 年最新）-

  1. 顺风 CSS 文档
  2. 纯编码
  3. 拉迪
  4. 技术代码

查看我的笔记

笔记



原文:

Firstly,

What is Tailwind CSS?

Tailwind is a CSS Framework created by Adam Wathan.

Unlike other frameworks, it does not come prebuilt with classes to add to the HTML tags.

Instead, it uses a different approach. It brings a much lower level of control by using utility-based classes.

You simply bring tiny components together to construct a UI that is unique.

** What Tailwind simply does is take a raw CSS File, processes this over a configuration file and produces and output. **

It is really helpful in creating responsive complex designs.

Check more on it's official website

Installation

Steps -

  • Make sure you have NodeJs installed in your system. If not download it here.

  • Write the following commands in the terminal opened in the specific location of the folder where to want to use/install it-

npm init -y
npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer vite
npx tailwindcss init -p
  • Create a CSS file and link it to your HTML file and edit it with the below piece of code- 
@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;

  • In your "tailwind.config.js" file, replace "content:[]" with "content:["*"]"

  • Add/edit the following line of JSON in your "package.json".

"scripts": { "start": "vite";
}
  • Now, finally write the following in your terminal to start a Development server for running Tailwind CSS-

Where to go now?

Now you are ready to get into Tailwind CSS. I have found some great resources for you all(latest 2022)-

  1. Tailwind CSS Docs
  2. Pure Coding
  3. Raddy
  4. Traversy Media
  5. TechCode

Check out my Notes

Notes

item.title: React 中的状态管理 | State management in React
item.description:
状态管理是每个应用程序最重要的方面之一。 简而言之，它是一种跨组件通信和共享数据的方式。应用程序的状态决定了用户看到的内容、应用程序的外观、存储的数据等等。

您需要在 React 应用程序中正确管理四种主要的状态类型。

  • 本地状态 本地状态是我们在一个或另一个组件中管理的数据。

    它通常在 React 中使用 useState 钩子进行管理。

  • 全局状态 全局状态是我们跨多个组件管理的数据。

    有推荐的库，如 Redux、Recoil、Jotai、Rematch、Zusand、Hookstate，在中小型应用程序中，我们可以使用 Context API 进行状态管理。

  • 服务器状态服务器状态是来自外部服务器的数据，必须与我们的 UI 状态集成。

    有诸如 React Query 和 SWR 之类的工具可以让管理服务器状态变得更加容易。

  • 如果您使用的是 React Router，则可以使用 useHistory 或 useLocation 获取所需的所有信息。 此外，如果您有任何需要使用的路由参数，例如根据数据获取数据，您可以使用 useParams 挂钩。

    如果您使用的是 Next.js，几乎所有内容都可以通过调用 useRouter 直接访问。

Onorio Catenacci - Onorio Catenacci - May 27 5月27日



原文:

State management is one of the most important aspects of every app. In a nutshell, it is a way to communicate and share the data across components The app’s state dictates what users see, how the app looks, what data is stored, and so on.

There are four main types of state you need to properly manage in React app.

  • Local state Local state is data we manage in one or another component.

    It is most often managed in React using the useState hook.

  • Global state Global state is data we manage across multiple components.

    There are the recommended libraries like Redux, Recoil, Jotai, Rematch, Zustand, Hookstate and in small to medium apps, we can use Context API for state management.

  • Server state Server state is data that comes from an external server that must be integrated with our UI state.

    There are tools such as React Query and SWR that make managing server state much easier.

  • URL state Data that exists on our URLs, including the pathname and query parameters. If you are using React Router, you can get all the information you need using useHistory or useLocation. Additionally, if you have any route parameters that you need to use, for example to fetch data based off of, you can use the useParams hook.

    If you are using Next.js, almost everything can access directly from calling useRouter.

Onorio Catenacci - May 27
